text: Hey everyone, today we're making a one-pan garlic butter shrimp pasta that comes together in about twenty minutes. Start by bringing a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the linguine until al dente. While that's going, pat the shrimp dry and season them with salt and pepper. Melt butter in a large skillet over medium heat, then add the garlic and cook until fragrant. Add the shrimp and cook for about two minutes per side until they turn pink. Pour in the white wine and lemon juice, and let it reduce for a minute. Add the cooked pasta straight into the skillet with a splash of pasta water. Finish with parmesan, fresh parsley, and a pinch of red pepper flakes. Serve immediately.

Add the shrimp and cook for about two minutes per side until they turn pink.
Pour in the white wine and lemon juice, and let it reduce for a minute.
Add the cooked pasta straight into the skillet with a splash of pasta water.
Finish with parmesan, fresh parsley, and a pinch of red pepper flakes. Serve immediately.
